description: TRIDENT attack/weakness/control graph data for Open Security Architecture

changes.description: CCI (Common Configuration Issues): 5,137 entries from DISA CCI XML ingestion
ASSESSES edges (1,877): cci -> control mapping from DISA CCI-to-NIST-800-53 crosswalk
CVE/NVD vulnerability ingestion: 53,957 vulnerabilities via NVD API v2
AFFECTS edges (59,077): vulnerability -> cpe product mapping from NVD
MISCONFIGURES edges (15,622): CCE weakness_class -> cpe platform mapping
CWE weakness catalog expanded from 135 to 944 entries via full MITRE CWE XML ingestion
TIBP: 29 infrastructure behavioral profiles with default weakness and protocol mappings
3 new edge types: PROFILE_DESCRIBES (30), DEFAULT_WEAKNESS (93), DEFAULT_USES_PROTOCOL (72)
explorerPlacement, graphKey, refFields, pseudoEdges added to trident-model.json (v1.1)
graph_version, graph_schema_version, graph_build_id added to metadata.json
ATLAS v5.4.0 merged: 155 AML.T techniques, 35 AML.M mitigations
Initial TRIDENT graph: 22 entity types, 38 edge types, 15K+ edges

deprecation.reason: Superseded by TRIDENT mitigation path (Control -> Mitigation -> Technique) which uses MITRE first-party data. CTID is a third-party interpretive mapping with 99.5% overlap with the mitigation path but lower authority.
deprecation.retained_for: backtesting, quality assurance, coverage validation against TRIDENT mitigation path

technique_control_mappings.notes: CTID maps 11 NIST families. Controls in other families (AT, AU, IR, MA, PE, PL, PM, PS, PT) have no ATT&CK mappings in this dataset. LEGACY: retained for validation only — the TRIDENT mitigation path (Control -PRESCRIBES-> Mitigation -COUNTERS-> Technique) provides equivalent coverage using MITRE first-party data.

mitigations.notes: 44 ATT&CK mitigations (M-series) with technique COUNTERS relationships. Cross-referenced to SP800-53 controls via shared technique coverage (PRESCRIBES edges). M1055 'Do Not Mitigate' correctly has no control links.

detection_strategies.source: MITRE ATT&CK STIX Data (v18 detection overhaul)
detection_strategies.notes: ATT&CK v18 replaced Data Sources with Detection Strategies (DET-series). Each strategy maps to one technique via DETECTS relationship.

cis_safeguard_index.notes: Reverse index of CIS v8 safeguard -> SP800-53 controls from existing OSA compliance mappings. Provides the IMPLEMENTED_BY edges in the TRIDENT model.

weakness_catalog.notes: 944 CWE weakness classes (expanded from 135 via full MITRE CWE XML ingestion). Mapped to ATT&CK techniques via CAPEC bridge + TRIDENT enrichment. Includes CCE entries with MISCONFIGURES edges to CPE products.

vulnerability_catalog.notes: 53,957 CVE vulnerabilities ingested from NVD API v2. Each vulnerability maps to CPE products via AFFECTS edges. Provides vulnerability-to-product impact analysis for the TRIDENT graph.

cce_integration.source: CCE (Common Configuration Enumeration) via CWE/weakness catalog
cce_integration.notes: CCE misconfiguration entries integrated as weakness_class entities with MISCONFIGURES edges to CPE products. CCE entries are identifiable by source='CCE' in the weakness catalog.

cci_catalog.source: DISA (Defense Information Systems Agency) CCI XML
cci_catalog.notes: 5,137 Common Configuration Issues (CCI) from DISA CCI XML. Each CCI maps to NIST 800-53 controls via ASSESSES edges. Provides compliance assessment granularity — CCI items are specific testable statements that evaluate whether a control is correctly implemented.

threat_actors.notes: 163 active ATT&CK threat groups (G-series intrusion-sets) with USES relationships to techniques. 9 actors excluded (revoked/deprecated or no technique overlap with our 469-technique catalog). Actors carry aliases but not country attribution (deferred).

process_capability_catalog.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
process_capability_catalog.notes: 30 process capability classes across 19 NIST 800-53 families. Each capability mapped to SP800-53 controls, CIS Safeguards, and ATT&CK Mitigations. Provides CLASSIFIED_BY edges connecting process implementations to canonical capability identifiers.

technology_capability_catalog.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
technology_capability_catalog.notes: 28 technology capability classes across 9 categories with 86 individual capabilities built on MITRE D3FEND. Each class mapped to SP800-53 controls, CIS Safeguards, and ATT&CK Mitigations. Provides PROVIDES_CAPABILITY edges connecting technology implementations to capability profiles.

adversary_capability_model.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
adversary_capability_model.notes: 5 adversary capability tiers (T1-T5) classifying all 469 ATT&CK techniques by minimum adversary sophistication required. Tiers are cumulative — higher tiers include all lower tier tradecraft. Uses STIX 2.1 vocabularies for actor attributes and NIST SP 800-30 capability/intent/targeting model. Technique assignments based on tool availability, skill requirement, detection difficulty, operational complexity, and prerequisite access.

human_factors_model.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
human_factors_model.notes: 24 cognitive vulnerability classes across 6 categories. Social engineering classes (AU/UR/TR/CU/HB) map to ATT&CK techniques via EXPLOITS_HUMAN edges with rationale. Insider disposition classes (IN) have no technique edges — they are risk factors mitigated by SP800-53 controls. Cialdini principle mapping on 17 of 24 classes; remaining 7 map to cognitive biases outside Cialdini framework.

data_classification_catalog.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
data_classification_catalog.notes: 14 data type classes across 8 categories with 4 classification tiers (DC-0 through DC-3). Each data type carries regulatory drivers, impact domains, and protection requirements grouped by data state (at-rest, in-transit, in-use, lifecycle, logging). Provides REQUIRES_PROTECTION edges (DataType -> Control) and PROTECTS_DATA edges (Control -> DataType) for threat-led pattern derivation.

cloud_service_catalog.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
cloud_service_catalog.notes: 22 cloud service entries across 7 providers and 11 service model categories. Each service carries shared responsibility splits — provider controls (inherited, customer does not implement) and customer controls (must implement, patterns derive from these). Replaces CPE for cloud services where traditional versioning and CVE models do not apply. Provides PROVIDER_IMPLEMENTS and CUSTOMER_IMPLEMENTS edges for threat-led pattern derivation with shared responsibility awareness.

protocol_catalog.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
protocol_catalog.notes: 37 protocol entries across 11 categories covering network transport, name resolution, authentication, file sharing, web/API, remote access, email/messaging, database, infrastructure, cloud/container, and industrial protocols. Each protocol carries ATT&CK technique references (TARGETS_PROTOCOL edges), secure alternative references (UPGRADES_TO edges), and SP800-53 control references. 3 ICS protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, BACnet) included with empty technique arrays — their techniques are ICS ATT&CK domain, not in the Enterprise catalog. SECURES_PROTOCOL edges (Control -> Protocol) deferred to separate design pass.

cpe_reference_catalog.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
cpe_reference_catalog.notes: Version-less CPE reference catalog (Option A). 4-layer model: L1-L3 general IT platforms, L4 financial services applications across 10 TFSP categories. OSA-minted CPEs for FS platforms absent from NVD carry source='osa'. 24 PARENT_OF hierarchy edges for product families. Designed as Tier 1 (fixed reference) of a two-tier architecture; Tier 2 (user runtime data) adds version-specific entries from asset inventory.

identity_domain_catalog.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
identity_domain_catalog.notes: 35 identity domain reference entries across 4 entity types: 10 IdentityProvider classes (enterprise directory, cloud IdP, federation broker, MFA, PAM, CIAM, CA/PKI, device trust, workload identity, NAC), 12 AccessPolicy classes (conditional access, RBAC, ABAC, SCP, credential, JIT, session, federation trust, network, data, API, lifecycle), 8 IdentityPrincipal classes (human interactive, human privileged, service account, managed identity, workload, device, API key, external), and 5 FederationPattern classes (hub-and-spoke, mesh, cascading, B2B, workload-to-cloud). NIST 800-63 assurance profiles (IAL, AAL, FAL) on identity providers. Provides SATISFIES edges (IDP/POL/PRI -> Control), MITIGATES edges (POL -> Technique), IMPLEMENTS_CLASS edges (IDP -> TTCE), USES_PROTOCOL edges (IDP -> TPE), and FEDERATES_TO edges (IDP -> IDP) for identity-aware threat-led pattern derivation.

fs_platform_catalog.source: OSA TFSP (Trident Financial Services Platform Types)
fs_platform_catalog.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
fs_platform_catalog.notes: 43 abstract financial services technology primitives across 10 categories. Each primitive represents a functional slot in an FS institution's architecture with assurance framework mappings. L4 FS CPE products map to TFSP types via INSTANCE_OF edges.

energy_platform_catalog.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
energy_platform_catalog.notes: 55 abstract energy sector technology primitives across 15 categories. Each primitive represents a functional slot in an energy organisation's architecture with Purdue level, safety classification, OT protocols, and applicable framework mappings. L4 energy CPE products map to TEPE types via INSTANCE_OF_ENERGY edges.

insurance_platform_catalog.source: OSA TISP (Trident Insurance Sector Platform Enumeration)
insurance_platform_catalog.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
insurance_platform_catalog.notes: 37 abstract insurance sector technology primitives across 10 categories. Each primitive represents a functional slot in an insurer's architecture with regulatory regime and assurance framework mappings. Provides PROFILE_EXPECTS_INSURANCE edges from vertical profiles to expected insurance platform types.

vertical_profile_catalog.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
vertical_profile_catalog.notes: 13 vertical profile entries defining expected platform types and adversary tier scoping for financial services and energy sector verticals. Provides PROFILE_EXPECTS_FS, PROFILE_EXPECTS_ENERGY, and PROFILE_SCOPES_TIER edges for vertical-specific threat-led pattern derivation.

zone_archetype_catalog.author: Chris Lethaby (Vinylwasp)
zone_archetype_catalog.notes: 20 zone archetype entries defining standard network zones with hosting relationships to platform types and control requirements. Provides ZONE_HOSTS, ZONE_HOSTS_ENERGY, and ZONE_REQUIRES edges for zone-based security architecture derivation.

atlas_ai_ml.source: MITRE ATLAS (Adversarial Threat Landscape for AI Systems)
atlas_ai_ml.notes: 155 ATLAS AI/ML techniques (AML.T-series) and 35 mitigations (AML.M-series) merged into existing technique and mitigation catalogs. All 35 mitigations mapped to SP 800-53 controls via design doc Section 4.3 (PRESCRIBES edges). 5 mitigations cross-referenced to ATT&CK equivalents via RELATED_TO edges. TACM tiers derived from ATLAS technique maturity levels. No ATLAS techniques at T1 — minimum is T2 (AI/ML-specific knowledge required).

trident_paths.direct_shortcut: Technique <-mitigates- Control (CTID, in control attack_techniques[] — LEGACY, retained for validation only)
trident_paths.data_classification_path: DataType -REQUIRES_PROTECTION-> Control / Control -PROTECTS_DATA-> DataType (TDCE regulatory-driven control derivation)
trident_paths.cloud_service_path: CloudService -PROVIDER_IMPLEMENTS-> Control / CloudService -CUSTOMER_IMPLEMENTS-> Control (TCSE shared responsibility derivation)
trident_paths.protocol_path: Technique -TARGETS_PROTOCOL-> Protocol (TPE protocol-specific attack surface derivation)
trident_paths.platform_hierarchy_path: CPE -PARENT_OF-> CPE (product family hierarchy — version-less parent to versioned child, or product family to variant)
trident_paths.fs_platform_instance_path: CPE -INSTANCE_OF-> FSPlatformType (L4 FS product to TFSP abstract technology primitive mapping)
trident_paths.energy_platform_instance_path: CPE -INSTANCE_OF_ENERGY-> EnergyPlatformType (L4 energy product to TEPE abstract technology primitive mapping)
trident_paths.vertical_profile_fs_path: VerticalProfile -PROFILE_EXPECTS_FS-> FSPlatformType (vertical profile to expected FS platform types)
trident_paths.vertical_profile_energy_path: VerticalProfile -PROFILE_EXPECTS_ENERGY-> EnergyPlatformType (vertical profile to expected energy platform types)
trident_paths.vertical_profile_tier_path: VerticalProfile -PROFILE_SCOPES_TIER-> AdversaryTier (vertical profile to relevant adversary capability tiers)
trident_paths.vertical_profile_insurance_path: VerticalProfile -PROFILE_EXPECTS_INSURANCE-> InsurancePlatformType (vertical profile to expected insurance platform types)
trident_paths.zone_hosting_path: ZoneArchetype -ZONE_HOSTS-> FSPlatformType / -ZONE_HOSTS_ENERGY-> EnergyPlatformType (zone to hosted platform types)
trident_paths.zone_control_path: ZoneArchetype -ZONE_REQUIRES-> Control (zone archetype to required security controls)
trident_paths.atlas_crossref_path: ATLAS Mitigation -RELATED_TO-> ATT&CK Mitigation (cross-framework equivalence for 5 shared mitigations)
trident_paths.behavioral_profile_path: InfrastructureBehavioralProfile -PROFILE_DESCRIBES-> CPE / -DEFAULT_WEAKNESS-> WeaknessClass / -DEFAULT_USES_PROTOCOL-> Protocol (TIBP infrastructure behavioral profiling)
trident_paths.cci_assessment_path: CCI -ASSESSES-> Control (DISA CCI testable statements to NIST 800-53 control assessment)

technique-catalog.json.description: 627 techniques (472 ATT&CK + 155 ATLAS AI/ML) with mitigations[], detection_strategies[], weaknesses[], actors[], minTier back-references

mitigation-catalog.json.description: 79 mitigations (44 ATT&CK + 35 ATLAS AI/ML) with techniques[], controls[], cis_safeguards[]

detection-catalog.json.description: 691 ATT&CK v18 detection strategies with techniques[]

cis-safeguard-index.json.description: 171 CIS v8 safeguards with controls[] reverse index

weakness-catalog.json.description: 944 CWE weakness classes (including CCE entries) with techniques[] and applicableCPEs[]

vulnerability-catalog.json.description: 53,957 CVE vulnerabilities from NVD API v2 with AFFECTS edges to CPE products

actor-catalog.json.description: 163 ATT&CK threat groups with techniques[], aliases

process-capability-catalog.json.description: 30 TPCE process capabilities with controlRefs[], cisSafeguards[], attackMitigations[]

technology-capability-catalog.json.description: 28 TTCE technology capabilities with 86 individual capabilities, controlRefs[], cisSafeguards[], attackMitigations[], D3FEND alignment

adversary-tier-catalog.json.description: 5 TACM adversary capability tiers with STIX/NIST attributes and technique tier distribution

human-factors-catalog.json.description: 24 THFM cognitive vulnerability classes across 6 categories with Cialdini mappings and SP800-53 control refs

data-classification-catalog.json.description: 14 TDCE data type classes across 8 categories with regulatory drivers, impact domains, and protection requirements

cloud-service-catalog.json.description: 22 TCSE cloud service entries across 7 providers with shared responsibility splits and customer/provider control mappings

protocol-catalog.json.description: 37 TPE protocol entries across 11 categories with technique references, secure alternatives, and SP800-53 control mappings

identity-domain-catalog.json.description: 35 TIDM identity domain entries: 10 identity providers, 12 access policies, 8 identity principals, 5 federation patterns with NIST 800-63 assurance profiles

cpe-catalog.json.description: 274 CPE reference entries across 4 layers: L1 Major Platforms (31), L2 Enterprise Apps (73), L3 Infrastructure (90), L4 FS Applications (80). 205 NVD-sourced, 69 OSA-minted. 10 TFSP sub-categories.

cpe-parent-of-edges.json.description: 24 CPE PARENT_OF hierarchy edges (product family relationships)

fs-platform-catalog.json.description: 43 TFSP financial services platform types across 10 categories with assurance framework mappings

insurance-platform-catalog.json.description: 37 TISP insurance sector platform types across 10 categories with regulatory regime and assurance framework mappings

vertical-profile-catalog.json.description: 13 vertical profile entries (FS and energy) with platform type expectations and TACM tier scoping

zone-archetype-catalog.json.description: 20 zone archetype entries with hosting relationships and control requirements

infrastructure-behavioral-profile-catalo.description: 29 TIBP infrastructure behavioral profiles with CPE, protocol, weakness, and control mappings

cci-catalog.json.description: 5,137 DISA CCI (Common Configuration Issues) with ASSESSES edges to NIST 800-53 controls

graph-edges.json.description: 92,378 explicit TRIDENT graph edges across 44 relationship types (includes ATLAS, vertical profile, zone archetype, insurance, platform instance, vulnerability AFFECTS, CCE MISCONFIGURES, and CCI ASSESSES edges)

trident-model.json.description: Single authoritative TRIDENT model definition — domains, node types, edge types, analytical paths

metadata.json.description: This file — provenance, version info, graph summary
